params.name: thread_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This corresponds to the value returned in the `id` field when you retrieve notifications (for example with the [`GET /notifications` operation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#list-notifications-for-the-authenticated-user)).

items.docs: Returns detailed information about a specific notification thread identified by the thread_id parameter. This endpoint retrieves the complete thread data including the notification subject, reason for the notification, repository information, and the current read/unread status. The operation requires authentication and will only return threads that belong to the authenticated user. The response includes metadata about when the thread was last updated and provides URLs for related operations such

params.name: thread_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This corresponds to the value returned in the `id` field when you retrieve notifications (for example with the [`GET /notifications` operation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#list-notifications-for-the-authenticated-user)).

items.docs: Marks a thread as "read." Marking a thread as "read" is equivalent to clicking a notification in your notification inbox on GitHub Enterprise Server: https://github.com/notifications.

info.name: GitHub Get Thread Subscription for the Authenticated User

params.name: thread_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This corresponds to the value returned in the `id` field when you retrieve notifications (for example with the [`GET /notifications` operation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#list-notifications-for-the-authenticated-user)).

items.docs:
This checks to see if the current user is subscribed to a thread. You can also [get a repository subscription](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/watching#get-a-repository-subscription).

Note that subscriptions are only generated if a user is participating in a conversation--for example, they've replied to the thread, were **@mentioned**, or manually subscribe to a thread.

params.name: thread_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This corresponds to the value returned in the `id` field when you retrieve notifications (for example with the [`GET /notifications` operation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#list-notifications-for-the-authenticated-user)).

items.docs:
If you are watching a repository, you receive notifications for all threads by default. Use this endpoint to ignore future notifications for threads until you comment on the thread or get an **@mention**.

You can also use this endpoint to subscribe to threads that you are currently not receiving notifications for or to subscribed to threads that you have previously ignored.

params.name: thread_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This corresponds to the value returned in the `id` field when you retrieve notifications (for example with the [`GET /notifications` operation](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#list-notifications-for-the-authenticated-user)).

items.docs: Mutes all future notifications for a conversation until you comment on the thread or get an **@mention**. If you are watching the repository of the thread, you will still receive notifications. To ignore future notifications for a repository you are watching, use the [Set a thread subscription](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.9/rest/activity/notifications#set-a-thread-subscription) endpoint and set `ignore` to `true`.
